description_long: This is a cordless electric drill with a robust, ergonomic design, primarily colored in green and black with red accent details. The drill features a pistol-grip handle with a textured, grippy surface for secure handling, and a large rectangular battery pack at the base, suggesting it is powered by a removable battery. The front of the drill is equipped with a black, cylindrical chuck holding a twist drill bit, and the body includes visible ventilation slots for cooling. A prominent trigger button is located on the handle, and additional red buttons or switches are present on the side, likely for operational controls. The overall construction appears to be a combination of hard plastic and metal, with a compact and sturdy build suitable for urban repair or construction tasks.
description: A green and black cordless electric drill with a twist bit, ergonomic handle, and visible battery pack, featuring red control accents and ventilation slots.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile, battery pack base, and the end of the handle.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full length of the drill, the twist bit, trigger button, and red control accents.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the handle, battery pack, and the top of the drill body.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite side of the drill, including the battery pack, handle, and additional red control details.
